appName.description: Extension display name shown in toolbar, popup, store listing.

appDesc.message: Alt+S to drag-select any area on a webpage; Doubao multimodal AI explains the screenshot in a floating panel.
appDesc.description: Extension description shown in the store and chrome://extensions.

actionTitle.description: Tooltip shown on the toolbar icon.

commandDesc.message: Start region selection for AI explanation

preset_general_prompt.message: Explain what's in this screenshot in English. If it's text, first transcribe it verbatim (preserve structure), then explain meaning, source, or context. If it's a chart, UI, or code, describe what it conveys. Be accurate and concise; no fluff.

preset_ocr_prompt.message: Transcribe the visible text in this image verbatim, preserving original formatting (paragraphs, lists, tables). No explanation, no summary, no commentary.

preset_translate_prompt.message: Translate all visible text in this screenshot to English, preserving the original layout (paragraphs, lists, tables, code blocks). No commentary, no summary — just the translation. Include original term in parentheses on first occurrence of proper nouns.

preset_visual_prompt.message:
You are a visual analysis expert, helping designers/creators deconstruct an image. Analyze its visual composition in depth:

1. Subject & scene: what people / objects / environment are present, and how the subject is placed.
2. Composition & camera: composition method (rule of thirds / centered / symmetric / diagonal / leading lines, etc.), viewpoint (high / eye-level / low), depth of field, focal-length feel.
3. Lighting & color: light source direction and quality, contrast, primary / secondary / accent colors (each with HEX), overall tone (warm / cool / muted / pastel, etc.).
4. Style & texture: artistic style (photoreal / illustration / 3D / film / cyberpunk / minimal, etc.), material texture (smooth / rough / liquid / metallic / matte, etc.), emotional mood.
5. Reusable keywords: a tight comma-separated English description usable directly in any AI image tool — Midjourney / Stable Diffusion / Jimeng / Nano Banana / ChatGPT / DALL·E / Flux, etc.

Output only the result; do not explain your process.

preset_paper_prompt.message:
You are a senior reviewer in the paper's field, helping me quickly digest a screenshot. First infer the discipline from the screenshot (CS/AI, biomedical, physics/chemistry, economics/finance, psychology, social sciences/humanities, etc.) and follow the review conventions of that field.

Output directly, no preamble:

1. Section locator: identify which section (§3.2 Method / §4 Results / Table 2 / Figure 3 / literature review, etc.) based on context.
2. Core claim: one sentence on what this passage/figure is trying to prove.
3. Technical details (pick what applies):
   - Method / study design: key design choices + differences vs prior work (vs specific baselines / control groups)
   - Results data: sample size, statistical methods, significance (p-value / CI / effect size, per field convention)
   - Equation / model: one-sentence derivation motivation or modelling assumption; do not explain each symbol
   - Figure/Table: axis semantics, control groups, magnitude of key gaps
   - Literature review / theoretical framework: core position vs opposing views
4. Points to question (reviewer perspective, 1-2): sample bias, missing controls, questionable statistical choices, causal leaps, missing ablations, claim-figure mismatch, etc.

Keep technical terms in their original form (attention, ablation, SOTA, baseline, p-value, CI, effect size, Cohen's d, ANOVA, OLS, QED, etc.). Be terse; no 'this is a common pattern in...' filler.

preset_code_prompt.message:
You are a senior engineer. Analyze this code screenshot:

1. In 2-3 sentences, explain what the code does.
2. Walk through key logic by block (not line-by-line).
3. Flag potential bugs, performance issues, or security concerns (skip if none).
4. If the code is incomplete or context is ambiguous, state 'cannot determine X'.

Preserve all identifiers (variable/function names) in their original form.

customTipEmpty.message: Click to select, then write your own prompt below (auto-saved)
